channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: The fourfold way to rupture in active solids
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02558-x

Quadrupolar topological defects act as organization centres for material failure in a biomimetic active nematic solid.
item.dc:title: The fourfold way to rupture in active solids
item.dc:creator: Daniel S. Seara
Vincenzo Vitelli

item.title: Anodic protection enables moisture-stable Mg<sub>3</sub>(Sb, Bi)<sub>2</sub> for thermoelectric cooling
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02563-0

Mg3(Sb, Bi)2 is a potential next-generation thermoelectric with cost-effectiveness compared with Bi2Te3; however, it suffers from poor moisture corrosion resistance. Here, an anodic protection strategy is presented that substantially reduces corrosion, enabling the 28-day performance of a module in high-temperature and high-humidity environment.
item.dc:title: Anodic protection enables moisture-stable Mg<sub>3</sub>(Sb, Bi)<sub>2</sub> for thermoelectric cooling
item.dc:creator: Zhiyuan Yu
Yuxin Sun
Haijun Wu
Fengkai Guo
Jin Hu
Ming Liu
Xianghong Zhou
Hao Wu
Jinsuo Hu
Lankun Wang
Yuke Zhu
Haoyang Tong
Jianbo Zhu
Zihang Liu
Wei Cai
Weishu Liu
Jiehe Sui

item.title: Materials with 5d electrons for future technologies
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02554-1

Materials with 5d electrons are receiving increasing attention due to their unique properties and functions. To understand property–function relations, this Perspective analyses six key factors in the electronic structure of 5d electron materials in a range of application areas.
item.dc:title: Materials with 5d electrons for future technologies
item.dc:creator: Lin Gu
Ce-Wen Nan
Yeqiang Tan

item.title: Mineral-originated bioelectronics for inhibition via lithium electrochemistry
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02526-5

A bioelectronic device incorporating triphylite (LiFePO4) as electrode is used for lithium-ion-specific inhibition of peripheral and central nervous system activities in rats and in mice.
item.dc:title: Mineral-originated bioelectronics for inhibition via lithium electrochemistry
item.dc:creator: Zhe Cheng
Tiantian Guo
Gangbin Yan
Jing Zhang
Jiping Yue
Chuanwang Yang
Suin Choi
Ananth Kamath
Saehyun Kim
Daniel S. Kohane
Chong Liu
Bozhi Tian

item.title: Narrowband helical emitter with frontier orbital confinement for stable deep-blue hybrid-tandem organic light-emitting diodes
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02529-2

Narrowband deep-blue organic light-emitting diodes combining high colour purity, efficiency and stability are realized by using multiresonance thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitters with a highly twisted helical structure.
item.dc:title: Narrowband helical emitter with frontier orbital confinement for stable deep-blue hybrid-tandem organic light-emitting diodes
item.dc:creator: Chuanqin Cheng
Minqiang Mai
Chenglong Li
Dongdong Zhang
Lian Duan

item.title: Designing disordered materials beyond equilibrium
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 26 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02552-3

A family of glassy materials emerges from local structural optimization, revealing an unexpected form of long-range orientational order.
item.dc:title: Designing disordered materials beyond equilibrium
item.dc:creator: Ludovic Berthier
M. D. Ediger

item.title: From a same origin to diverse and recyclable materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 26 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02550-5

A single monomer can be leveraged to generate polymeric materials with diverse structures or tunable properties and chemical recyclability.
item.dc:title: From a same origin to diverse and recyclable materials
item.dc:creator: Wei-Feng Zheng
Eugene Y.-X. Chen

item.title: Entanglement made measurable
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 25 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02557-y

A resonant inelastic X-ray scattering interferometric approach exposes a highly entangled electronic ground state in a pyrochlore iridate, linking quantum entanglement to intertwined symmetry-breaking orders in a correlated metal near a metal–insulator transition.
item.dc:title: Entanglement made measurable
item.dc:creator: Riccardo Arpaia
